ai_chat.input.placeholder: Type a question, press Enter to send
ai_chat.key_saved: API Key saved. You can start chatting now.
ai_chat.no_key_prompt: Please configure your Anthropic API Key first before using AI Bot.
ai_chat.no_key_warn: No API Key found. Please click 「Config Key」 first.
ai_chat.welcome: Hi, I'm AI Bot. Feel free to ask about Jetson, Skills, environment setup, or troubleshooting.
ai_chat.tool.rejected: [Command rejected: only read-only diagnostic commands are allowed, writes/deletes are not permitted]
ai_chat.inject.skill:
Tell me about this Skill: **{name}**

Description: {desc}{cmds}
ai_chat.inject.topic:
Tell me about this item: {title}

Summary: {summary}{detail}
ai_chat.inject.error:
An error occurred while running 「{title}」. Here is the error log — please analyze and suggest a fix:

```
{snippet}
```
ai_chat.system: You are the AI assistant for Seeed Jetson Develop Tool, specialized in development, configuration, and troubleshooting for NVIDIA Jetson boards. You are familiar with Jetson Nano, Orin Nano, Orin NX, and related technology such as JetPack, L4T, CUDA, TensorRT, and ROS. Keep answers concise; use code blocks for commands. Always respond in English. When the user encounters an error and a device is connected, you may call the run_ssh_command tool to execute read-only diagnostic commands on the Jetson (docker logs, journalctl, df, free, cat log files, etc.) and suggest solutions based on the real output.
ai_chat.system.skills_header: Available Skills (for reference; users can run them on the Skills page):
ai_chat.system.apps_header: Available Demo Apps (users can install/run them from App Marketplace):
ai_chat.tool_def.description: Execute a command on the connected Jetson device via SSH to help diagnose and resolve issues. Can run install, configure, query, and service management commands. Destructive commands (rm -rf /, dd write to disk, mkfs format, etc.) are forbidden.
ai_chat.tool_def.cmd_desc: The shell command to execute on the Jetson
ai_chat.tool_def.reason_desc: Purpose of this command (brief explanation shown to the user)
